title: Sour and tender with kaffir-lime-leaf Filipino Dish Comal-cooked rice
description: A sour and tender Filipino dish featuring kaffir-lime-leaf, perfect for a monsoon evening. This recipe is sugar-free-friendly and can be easily adapted to suit your taste.
instructions: Heat a comal or a large skillet over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and saut9 the onion until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and saut9 for another minute.
Add the tomatoes and cook until they start to break down, about 5 minutes.
Add the kaffir lime leaves and vinegar, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the rice and water to the comal, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the comal.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the rice with a fork and season with salt to taste.
